title: Communication operators
description: Apply electrical and electronic theory and related knowledge, usually under the direction of engineering staff, to design, build, repair, adjust, and modify electrical components, circuitry, controls, and machinery for subsequent evaluation and use by engineering staff in making engineering design decisions. Set up, maintain, and dismantle audio and video equipment, such as microphones, sound speakers, connecting wires and cables, sound and mixing boards, video cameras, video monitors and servers, and related electronic equipment for live or recorded events, such as concerts, meetings, conventions, presentations, podcasts, news conferences, and sporting events. Set up, operate, and maintain the electronic equipment used to acquire, edit, and transmit audio and video for radio or television programs. Control and adjust incoming and outgoing broadcast signals to regulate sound volume, signal strength, and signal clarity. Operate satellite, microwave, or other transmitter equipment to broadcast radio or television programs. (Based on 19 related US occupations.)

tasks.task: Observe signal lights on switchboards, and dial or press buttons to make connections.
Answer incoming calls, greeting callers, providing information, transferring calls or taking messages as necessary, and relay or route written or verbal messages.

Page individuals to inform them of telephone calls, using paging or interoffice communication equipment, and operate telephone switchboards and systems to advance and complete connections, including local, long distance, and emergency calls.
Record messages, suggesting rewording for clarity or conciseness, and keep records of calls placed and charges incurred.
Listen to customer requests, referring to directories to answer questions and provide telephone information, and update directory information.
Use communication systems, such as telephone, switchboard, intercom, or public address.

Perform clerical duties such as typing, proofreading, and sorting mail, and keep records of work performed. Additionally, perform various data entry and word processing tasks, such as updating phone directories and creating schedules.
Greet visitors, log them in and out of the facility, and assign them security badges.

Monitor emergency and code alarms, make emergency announcements, or route emergency calls to the appropriate location, and offer special assistance to persons unable to dial or in emergency situations.
Set up, test, and adjust recording equipment for recording sessions and live performances, and operate television or motion picture cameras to record scenes for television broadcasts or advertising.
Install broadcast equipment, troubleshoot equipment problems, and perform maintenance or minor repairs using hand tools.
Perform administrative tasks such as scheduling appointments, handling incoming or outgoing mail, and managing petty cash.
Prepare for recording sessions by selecting and setting up microphones, and regulate volume level and sound quality during recording sessions using control consoles.
Convert video and audio recordings into digital formats for editing or archiving, and compress, digitize, duplicate, and store audio and video data.

Control the lights and sound of events such as live concerts, before and after performances, and during intermissions; mix and edit voices, music, and sound effects for live performances and prerecorded events using sound mixing boards.
Monitor strength, clarity, and reliability of incoming and outgoing signals, adjust equipment as necessary to maintain quality broadcasts, and observe monitors to determine audio and video levels.
Set up, operate, and maintain broadcast station computers and networks, and design layouts of audio and video equipment and perform upgrades and maintenance.
Notify supervisors when major equipment repairs are needed and report equipment problems ensuring that repairs are made.
Perform minor repairs and routine cleaning of audio and video equipment, including testing microphones to ensure proper working condition.
Maintain programming logs as required and observe and adjust audio and video levels to ensure quality broadcasts.
Collect money from customers, make change, and record transactions on customer receipts.
Listen to and resolve customers' complaints regarding products or services.
Inform regular customers of new products or services and price changes.
Maintain records, such as vehicle logs, records of cargo, or billing statements, in accordance with regulations.
Create electronics drawings or graphics representing industrial control, instrumentation, sensors, or telecom networks, using computer-aided design (CAD) software.
Procure parts and maintain inventory and documentation.
Modify electrical prototypes, parts, assemblies, or systems to correct functional deviations and ensure proper functioning of electronics equipment or systems.
Research equipment or component needs and sources, considering pricing and operational costs.
Specify, coordinate, or conduct quality control or assurance programs or procedures.
Supervise the installation or operation of electronic equipment or systems and provide user applications or engineering support for new or existing equipment.
Modify, assemble, test, or maintain electronic components or circuitry according to engineering instructions, using hand or power tools.

Set up and operate specialized or standard test equipment to diagnose, test, or analyze electronic components or systems, and ensure adherence to design specifications and compliance with electrical codes.
Identify and resolve equipment malfunctions, working with manufacturers or field representatives as necessary to procure replacement parts.
Participate in training or education activities to stay updated on engineering or industry advances.
Drive vehicles or operate equipment to complete work assignments, maintaining knowledge of first-aid procedures and ensuring safety regulations are followed.
Prepare and submit reports on work activities, operations, production, or any incidents that occur during operations.
Confer with customers, supervisors, or other personnel to resolve problems or provide information about services or products.
Report any vehicle malfunctions or needed repairs, and maintain up-to-date knowledge of operational safety regulations.
Examine, measure, or weigh cargo or materials to determine specific handling requirements.
Participate in continuing education to stay abreast of industry trends and developments.
